name: Later
price_basis: Starter at $25 monthly billing, $18.75 per month billed yearly, checked 2026-07
verdict_reason: KILL: $300 a year to post on time. Zo runs the same queue from a file you edit and never meters your posts.
what_zo_builds: A visual-first queue: a folder of media with captions and send times in front matter, a cron publisher, and a preview page that renders the upcoming grid before anything ships. Rescheduling is cut and paste in a file, not a paywalled calendar, and there is no per-post meter deciding when your month is over.
dont_kill_if: Instagram is your storefront and you depend on Later's link in bio page and auto-publish partnerships. Rebuilding those pieces has real friction on Meta's side of the fence.
build_brief: Goal: replace my Later Starter plan with a media queue I own. Export upcoming posts, captions, and media. Create a /queue folder where each post is a media file plus a caption and send time. Build the cron publisher and a preview route that renders the upcoming grid so I can check the week at a glance. Ship a week of posts in parallel with Later, verify timing and formatting, then cancel. Done when the grid preview matches what actually posts and the $25 charge stops.
